title: Making a change — How easy is it to change our lives?
content_html:
Making a change

How easy is it for us to change our lives — and why?

In 1990, a young American named Christopher McCandless gave up his career plans, left behind everyone he knew, including his family, and went off on an adventure. He was 22 at the time. In an act of kindness, he donated all his savings to the famous charity, Oxfam International, and hitchhiked his way through America to Alaska. His decisions were so unusual for his age that Jon Krakauer wrote a book about them called Into the Wild, and Sean Penn directed a film that had the same title.

Of course, this is an unusual story. Most college graduates would not do quite the same thing. However, studies do show that in teenage years, people are more likely to try out new experiences. Instead of following the family career path, for example, and working his way up the same organisation like his grandfather did, a 15-year-old may dream about becoming a traveller — only to find in his early 20s that this fascination with new places is declining and change is less attractive. This age-related trend can be observed in all cultures.

The reason why people all over the world become less keen to change as they get older may be because people’s lives generally follow similar patterns and involve similar demands. Most people, wherever they are, aim to find a job and a partner. As they get older, they may have young children to look after and possibly elderly family members. These responsibilities cannot be achieved without some degree of consistency, which means that new experiences and ideas may not have a place in the person’s life. New experiences may bring excitement but also insecurity, and so most people prefer to stay with the familiar.

However, not every individual is the same. One toddler may want to play a different game every day and get fed up if nothing changes at the nursery. Another may seek out and play with the same children and toys on every visit. Young children who avoid new experiences will grow up to be more conventional than others. Psychologists argue that those who have more open personalities as children are more open than others might be when they are older. They also suggest that young men have a greater interest in novelty than women, although, as they age, this desire for new experiences fades more quickly than it does in women.

The truth is that, as we get older, we prefer the things we know. We tend to order the same meals in restaurants, sit on the same side of the train when we commute to work, go on holiday to the same places and construct our day in the same way. If you are older than 20, remember that your openness to new experiences is slowly declining. So you are better off making a new start today than postponing it until later.

questions.question: Teenagers are more ready to have new experiences than young adults.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 1–6

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 1–6 on your answer sheet, write
YES  if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO  if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN  if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this
questions.explanation: EN: 'studies do show that in teenage years, people are more likely to try out new experiences' compared to their early 20s. VI: 'các nghiên cứu thực sự chỉ ra rằng ở độ tuổi thiếu niên, con người có nhiều khả năng thử các trải nghiệm mới hơn' so với tuổi ngoài 20.

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question: Grandparents usually encourage their grandchildren to get a well-paid job.
questions.explanation: EN: The text mentions a grandfather working up an organization but does not discuss whether grandparents encourage grandchildren to get well-paid jobs. VI: Văn bản đề cập đến người ông làm việc thăng tiến trong tổ chức nhưng không thảo luận về việc ông bà có khuyến khích cháu tìm công việc lương cao hay không.

questions.question: Life demands are different depending on which country you live in.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states: 'The reason... may be because people's lives generally follow similar patterns and involve similar demands' and this 'can be observed in all cultures'. VI: Văn bản nêu rõ: 'Lý do... có thể là vì cuộc sống con người nhìn chung theo các khuôn mẫu tương tự và bao gồm các nhu cầu tương tự nhau' và điều này 'có thể được quan sát ở mọi nền văn hóa'.

questions.question: Some toddlers find repetitive activities boring.
questions.explanation: EN: 'One toddler may want to play a different game every day and get fed up if nothing changes' (repetitive tasks are boring). VI: 'Một đứa trẻ chập chững có thể muốn chơi trò khác mỗi ngày và chán ngấy nếu không có gì thay đổi' (hoạt động lặp lại là nhàm chán).

questions.question: Children who dislike new experiences become more adventurous as adults.
questions.explanation: EN: 'Young children who avoid new experiences will grow up to be more conventional than others'. VI: 'Trẻ nhỏ tránh các trải nghiệm mới lớn lên sẽ sống theo khuôn mẫu thông thường hơn những người khác'.

questions.question: If you want to change something in your life, you should avoid delay.
questions.explanation: EN: 'So you are better off making a new start today than postponing it until later' (avoid delay). VI: 'Vì vậy bạn tốt hơn hết nên có khởi đầu mới ngay hôm nay thay vì trì hoãn nó cho đến sau này' (tránh trì hoãn).

questions.answer: D. to give money or goods to a person or organisation that needs help
questions.question: donate
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 7–12

Match each statement with the correct option, A–F.

Write the correct letter in boxes 7–12 on your answer sheet.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: donate.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: "...he donated all his savings to the famous charity, Oxfam International..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Trong văn cảnh, Christopher đã "quyên góp" toàn bộ tiền tiết kiệm cho một tổ chức từ thiện. Điều này trùng khớp hoàn toàn với định nghĩa d: đưa tiền hoặc hàng hóa cho một người hoặc tổ chức cần giúp đỡ (to give money or goods to a person or organisation that needs help).
Đáp án: D

**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.
featureOptions: having no confidence in yourself and what you can do
the quality of being new or unusual
willing to consider a discussion, suggestion, experience, etc.
to give money or goods to a person or organisation that needs help
traditional and not willing to try new ideas
when someone always behaves or performs in a similar way

questions.questionHtml:
List of features

A  having no confidence in yourself and what you can do

B  the quality of being new or unusual

C  willing to consider a discussion, suggestion, experience, etc.

D  to give money or goods to a person or organisation that needs help

E  traditional and not willing to try new ideas

F  when someone always behaves or performs in a similar way

7_______ donate

8_______ consistency

9_______ insecurity

10_______ conventional

11_______ novelty

12_______ openness (to)

questions.question: consistency
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: consistency.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: "These responsibilities cannot be achieved without some degree of consistency, which means that new experiences and ideas may not have a place..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Văn cảnh nói về việc cuộc sống cần sự ổn định, duy trì đều đặn để gánh vác trách nhiệm. Điều này trùng khớp với định nghĩa f: khi ai đó luôn hành xử hoặc thực hiện theo một cách tương tự nhau (when someone always behaves or performs in a similar way).
Đáp án:

questions.question: insecurity
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: insecurity.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: "New experiences may bring excitement but also insecurity, and so most people prefer to stay with the familiar."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Rủi ro từ trải nghiệm mới mang lại sự "bấp bênh, thiếu an toàn" khiến người ta e ngại. Nó tương ứng với định nghĩa a: không có sự tự tin vào bản thân và những gì mình có thể làm (having no confidence in yourself and what you can do).
Đáp án:

questions.question: conventional
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: conventional.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4: "Young children who avoid new experiences will grow up to be more conventional than others."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Những đứa trẻ né tránh trải nghiệm mới lớn lên sẽ trở nên "truyền thống, rập khuôn" hơn. Từ này trùng khớp với định nghĩa e: truyền thống và không sẵn sàng thử các ý tưởng mới (traditional and not willing to try new ideas).
Đáp án:

questions.question: novelty
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: novelty.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4: "They also suggest that young men have a greater interest in novelty than women..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Đàn ông trẻ tuổi thường có hứng thú lớn hơn với "sự mới lạ". Trùng khớp với định nghĩa b: tính chất của sự mới mẻ hoặc khác lạ (the quality of being new or unusual).
Đáp án:

questions.question: openness (to)
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: openness (to).
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 5: "...remember that your openness to new experiences is slowly declining."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Sự "cởi mở, sẵn sàng tiếp nhận" đối với các trải nghiệm mới giảm dần theo thời gian. Từ này trùng khớp với định nghĩa c: sẵn sàng cân nhắc một cuộc thảo luận, gợi ý, trải nghiệm, v.v. (willing to consider a discussion, suggestion, experience, etc.).
Đáp án:
